notes:
Terrain rendering layer added to WorldMapScene.

## Biome verification
Read src/renderer/game/data/regions.js and confirmed every region carries a `biome:` field tagged before this run (arizona=DESERT, andes=MOUNTAIN, arabian=DESERT, turkish=GRASSLAND, persian=ROCK, swahili=WATER, chinese=UNKNOWN, pakistan=MOUNTAIN, malay=UNKNOWN). The BIOME enum is exported from regions.js and is imported by WorldMapScene.

## Edits made (line ranges)
- Lines 13-46: Added `import { BIOME } from '../data/regions.js'`, the `BIOME_COLORS` palette constant, the `WORLD_REGION_BIOME` bridge map, and the `biomeForWorldRegion()` helper at module scope.
- Lines 75-86: Added the `_renderTerrainLayer({...})` invocation in `create()`, placed AFTER the parchment rectangle and BEFORE the title, so terrain is the very first painted layer above the dark background and parchment.
- Lines 219-289: Added the `_renderTerrainLayer` method itself. Allocates a single `_terrainContainer` at depth -100 (strictly bottom), paints three concentric translucent circles per location for soft-edged blobs (~225px outer radius on a 1024px-wide canvas), adds deterministic stipple dots only for desert blobs, and clips the whole layer to the parchment rectangle via a geometry mask so blobs don't bleed onto the dark border.

## Layer-order verification
Final scene render order is now: dark background rect â†’ parchment rect â†’ terrain container (depth -100, but added BEFORE every other interactive object so the explicit -100 just hardens what the natural creation order already gives) â†’ title/subtitle text â†’ connection lines (paths) â†’ location node circles â†’ icons â†’ name labels â†’ difficulty stars â†’ quest indicators â†’ home node â†’ back button â†’ legend. Terrain sits strictly beneath paths, nodes, and labels.

## Click handlers
Not touched. The location iteration loop, `circle.setInteractive(...)`, the `pointerdown`/`pointerover`/`pointerout` registrations, and the `_selectLocation` / `_showLockHint` flow are byte-identical to the pre-edit file. The terrain graphics object never receives `setInteractive` and is held in a depth-pinned container, so input events still hit the node circles unchanged.

## Bridge between regions.js and worldMapData.js
The local-area world map in WorldMapScene uses worldMapData.js REGIONS (arizona_desert, mountain_range), which are different ids from the cross-cultural regions.js (arizona, andes, arabian, ...). Per the data-layer scope rule (regions.js / worldMapData.js are owned by world-biome-classifier), I did NOT add a `biome:` field to worldMapData.js REGIONS. Instead I added a small inline `WORLD_REGION_BIOME` map inside WorldMapScene to bridge the two: arizona_desertâ†’DESERT, mountain_rangeâ†’MOUNTAIN. Future expansion is a one-line addition.

## Overlap notes for future tuning
- All three current desert_foraging / copper_mine / salt_river locations sit in the arizona_desert region, so they all paint the same DESERT color and their blobs heavily overlap â€” this is intentional given they share a biome and the overlap reads as a single contiguous desert. Once non-Sonoran locations land, the overlap will need re-tuning (probably a smaller baseR, or per-region clustering).
- The home/neighborhood node at (0.15 * usableW, 0.2 * usableH + 20) currently has no terrain blob underneath it â€” it floats on the parchment. world-fog-overlay or a later pass may want to add a small URBAN blob there.
- baseR is `max(usableW, usableH) * 0.22` â‰ˆ 225px on a 1024Ã—768 map, matching the spec's 200-300px guidance.

## Pre-existing dirty audio block â€” explicit acknowledgment
Lines 212-216 of the post-edit file (which were lines 169-176 of the pre-edit file) contain the audio block:
```
// Audio â€” goes through SCENE_MUSIC so the file-backed track for this
// scene is used (desert_discovery had no real audio file and killed the
// music every time the player opened the map).
const audioMgr = this.registry.get('audioManager');
audioMgr?.transitionToScene('WorldMapScene');
```
This is the pre-existing uncommitted edit that was already in the working tree before my dispatch. I preserved it verbatim and did NOT modify it. It is NOT my work â€” it will appear in the reviewer's `git diff HEAD` for WorldMapScene.js but should be attributed to the prior author. My own changes are isolated to the import block (lines 13-46), the call site in create() (lines 75-86), and the `_renderTerrainLayer` method (lines 219-289).
